role_description: Senior Instrument Technician supervising a crew of instrument techs and junior techs on industrial construction or refinery projects. Manages daily work assignments for instrument installation, calibration, loop checking, and troubleshooting. Ensures proper hook-up drawing compliance, calibration accuracy, and reports crew progress to the instrument lead.
report_focus: When structuring this person's reports, look for: crew status and headcount, work assignments given, installation progress, calibrations performed, loop checks completed, material and tool needs, coordination with other trades, quality issues found, safety observations, problems escalated from techs, and plans for tomorrow.
safety_rules: All personnel must wear required PPE at all times: hard hat, safety glasses, steel-toe boots, FR clothing
Never work on energized or pressurized systems without proper isolation and LOTO procedures
Confined space entry requires a permit, atmospheric testing, and a dedicated attendant
Hot work requires a permit and a fire watch for minimum 30 minutes after work completion
Fall protection is required at heights of 6 feet or more — 100% tie-off at all times
Verify zero energy state before working on any instrument loop
Use proper PPE when handling calibration gases — check SDS
Never bypass safety instrumented systems (SIS) without proper authorization
Instrument air systems must be properly isolated before maintenance
Use only approved intrinsically safe equipment in classified areas
Verify area classification before using any non-IS equipment
Report all incidents, near-misses, and unsafe conditions immediately
Attend daily toolbox talks and JSA reviews before starting work
Maintain 3 points of contact when climbing ladders
Keep work areas clean and organized
